step.title: Step 20
step.actionDisplay: RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 6 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 99, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'moves player 3px right, leaves colored trail (VERIFIED step 2)', 'UP': 'moves player 3px up, leaves colored trail (VERIFIED step 3)', 'LEFT': 'moves player 3px left, leaves colored trail (VERIFIED step 5)', 'DOWN': 'moves player 3px down, leaves colored trail (VERIFIED step 14)', 'SPACE': 'teleports player to other cross center (VERIFIED step 4)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'black_bg': 'background', 'gray_square': '3x3 charcoal container with colored dot at center', 'yellow_cross': 'large yellow cross centered near (9,10)-(20,9)', 'blue_cross': 'blue cross centered near (36,45)', 'purple_bar': 'bottom HUD progress bar at row 63', 'white_pixel': 'player character, leaves trail matching current cross color', 'yellow_dot': '1px yellow dot inside gray square', 'blue_dot': '1px blue dot inside gray square'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_purple_bar': 'progress/step counter that shrinks as actions are taken'}
- Win pattern: Collect all yellow dots by drawing trail through them, then SPACE to blue cross and collect blue dots
Current level:
- World model: Player at (9,9). Yellow dots remaining: (3,15), (9,24), (17,15). (9,6) was collected! Yellow cross vertical bar at col 9-10, horizontal bar at row 9-10. The cross center is near (9,10).
- Goal model: Collect remaining yellow dots (3,15), (9,24), (17,15), then SPACE to blue cross for blue dots
- Recent findings: ['(9,6) collected when player moved LEFT through it (VERIFIED step 82) - trail from cross horizontal bar collected it', 'Yellow cross center near (9,10), horizontal bar at row ~9, vertical bar at col ~10', 'Trail on row 9 is yellow from cross horizontal bar', 'Gray squares block trail when player is inside them but trail from cross bar can collect dots']
- Open questions: ['How to collect (17,15) which is not on 3px grid from player position?', 'How to collect (3,15) - need to approach from cross direction?', "How to collect (9,24) - it's on row 9 same as cross horizontal bar"]
- Plan: From (9,9): RIGHT x8 to (9,33) passing through (9,24) to collect it. Then LEFT back to cross, go UP/DOWN to collect (3,15) and (17,15).
- Strategy log: ['Landing on dots does not collect them', 'Moving RIGHT from inside gray square does not draw trail (step 82)', 'Moving LEFT through dot from cross direction collects it (step 82) - (9,6) was collected']
- Failed probes: ['landing_on_dot_at_(9,6): no collection (step 37)', 'landing_on_dot_at_(3,15): no collection (step 42)', 'RIGHT from (9,6) inside gray square: no trail drawn (step 82)', 'LEFT through (9,6): collected (9,6) but not (3,15) or (17,15) (step 82)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check the current state after RIGHT x6 from (9,6) to (9,24). I need to see if (9,24) was collected and what dots remain.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find yellow dots (1px yellow objects)
yellow_dots = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        r, c = n['boundary'][0]
        yellow_dots.append((r, c))

# Find player
player_pos = None
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        r, c = n['boundary'][0]
        player_pos = (r, c)

print(f"Player: {player_pos}")
print(f"Yellow dots: {sorted(yellow_dots)}")

# Check row 9 around col 24
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Row 9 cols 20-30: '{lines[9][20:30]}'")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "moves 3px right, leaves colored trail", "UP": "moves 3px up, leaves colored trail", "LEFT": "moves 3px left, leaves colored trail", "DOWN": "moves 3px down, leaves colored trail", "SPACE": "teleports to other cross center"}, "object_taxonomy": {"black_bg": "background", "gray_square": "3x3 charcoal container with colored dot at center", "yellow_cross": "large yellow cross centered near (9,9)", "blue_cross": "blue cross centered near (36,45)", "white_pixel": "player"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_purple_bar": "progress counter"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": ""}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Player moved RIGHT x6 from (9,6). Need to check if (9,24) was collected.", "goal_model": "Collect remaining yellow dots, then SPACE for blue dots", "recent_findings": ["(9,6) collected by landing on it"], "open_questions": ["Was (9,24) collected by RIGHT trail?"], "current_plan": "Check state, then collect remaining dots", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
